biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — present in ~60% of tool-using units: 781e356c-0 (rg compounds via Bash throughout; grep -vi via Bash pipe filter); 781e356c-2 (grep -E pipe chains via Bash for comment-line filtering; python3 inline JSON chained with gh pr view); 781e356c-3 (python3 inline JSON via Bash for mcp-catalogue inspection x3 calls before locating correct file); 1c86d512-3 (find/ls compounds via Bash; sips via Bash instead of dedicated tool); 1c86d512-12 (awk NR-range slicing via Bash instead of Read with offset+limit; grep -E via Bash); 1c86d512-20 (sed -n NR,NR via Bash as Read substitute — new sub-variant not previously catalogued; awk range extraction via Bash; grep -E/-v via Bash throughout); thirtieth+ consecutive cycle above 50%, no downward trajectory; sed -n-as-Read-substitute is a new sub-variant to add to known catalog

bash_retry_without_diagnosis:RECURRING:STABLE — 3 units: 781e356c-0 (3 consecutive near-identical rg compound searches fail before AskUserQuestion pivot at position 18 — each search broadens pattern slightly without diagnostic pwd/ls step); 1c86d512-3 (iconutil -c iconset fails x2 with wrong output extension suffix before correct .iconset suffix succeeds); 8088634a-13 (propose.sh Bash call blocked x1 by hook false-positive; Write+Edit to project memory path fail x3 before hook-feedback pivot then /tmp staging workaround); 3 units at low end of prior 4-6 unit baseline — possible marginal improvement

compound_command_overreach:RECURRING:STABLE — 3 units: 781e356c-0 (6+ Bash calls with compound rg+ls+grep patterns across multiple targets before AskUserQuestion; includes grep -vi Bash pipe compound on rg output); 781e356c-2 (git diff + grep -E pipe chains x2 built inline for comment-line filtering instead of scripted approach; python3 inline JSON parsing via Bash for gh output); 8088634a-8 (build+kill+sleep+open+pgrep+5-iteration ps-sampling loop in single Bash call; 300-sample awk bubble-sort stats pipeline inline); 3 units at prior 3-4 unit plateau

redundant_read_same_unit:RECURRING:STABLE — 2 units: 1c86d512-11 (Read daemon.rs x3 at positions 2, 4, 6 with no offset variation between second and third reads — pattern structurally matches prior task_runner.py/-51 recurrence on a different file); 1c86d512-12 (Read weekly_briefing.rs x3 at positions 13, 16, 17 — second and third reads with no offset variation between them; no new information retrieved by repeated full reads); 2 units within prior 2-4 unit range

git_push_approval_assumed_from_prior_context:RECURRING — 1 unit: 8088634a-14 (git commit + git push origin main citing 'pre-approved per per-repo policy' — a self-constructed session-scope allowlist not backed by any persistent approval mechanism or in-turn confirmation; no AskUserQuestion or re-verification before push; directly contradicts rule requiring fresh approval per push; dream-insight (conf=0.72) flags this exact failure mode across 91 sessions; the 'per-repo policy' framing is an invented exception to the hard rule)

hook_false_positive_content_match:NEW:FIRST_OCCURRENCE — 1 unit: 8088634a-13 (cli-gating PreToolUse hook blocks bash ~/.claude/scripts/propose.sh add because the --body argument heredoc contained the literal substring 'gh repo create' — hook matched argv-as-substring on full command line including quoted args, not on the actual command being invoked; agent correctly identified as false-positive via hook-feedback.sh rather than circumventing; worked around by staging body to /tmp then re-invoking; this is a new pattern — hook generating false blocks on legitimate non-gh commands due to arg-content substring matching — distinct from agent bypassing hooks)

grep_hook_bypass:RECURRING:POSSIBLY_IMPROVING — 0 units with direct grep-via-Bash observed as primary pattern; 781e356c-0 grep -vi appears as Bash pipe filter on rg output (borderline); no /usr/bin/ prefix circumvention in any unit; 1c86d512-20 uses grep -E in Bash compounds but integrated with cargo commands rather than standalone; possible marginal improvement from prior 4-6 unit plateau — below prior high-water marks
cross_references: 8088634a-14 VIOLATES dream-insight (conf=0.72, 2026-05-07 17:03 UTC): 'Always re-verify git push approval after any compaction or context restoration event, treating compaction as an approval-reset boundary.' Unit cites 'pre-approved per per-repo policy' from earlier in session without re-confirmation in this turn. The insight explicitly frames approval-state evaporation at compaction boundaries; the same failure applies within long sessions where the granting turn is many turns prior and context has been partially replaced by summarization.

781e356c-3 CONFIRMS prior calibration note re render MCP: mcp__render__select_workspace fails on first call (get_selected_workspace), then select_workspace succeeds, followed by list_services, list_postgres_instances, query_render_postgres all succeeding. Consistent with prior calibration observation that '781e356c-2 and -3 show IMPROVED render MCP handling in new session context — improvement is session-scoped not agent-wide.' This batch's unit-3 from the same session (781e356c) confirms the session-scoped success pattern holds across unit boundaries within the same session.

8088634a-13 PARTIALLY CONFIRMS dream-insight (conf=0.85, 2026-05-08) about self-correcting after hook blocks: agent correctly routes through hook-feedback.sh and /tmp workaround rather than abandoning or bypassing. The memory-write failures (Write+Edit to project memory path fail x3) are path-configuration issues not hook issues — resolved by correct path after hook-feedback clarification. Memory deduplication concern from dream-insight does not apply here (new distinct entry, not a duplicate).

1c86d512-20 git push to master BLOCKED (hook fires correctly) vs 8088634a-14 git push to main SUCCEEDS (citing prior approval): the asymmetry across these two units from different sessions illustrates the dream-insight observation that approval state is conversational — the i-dream session had no prior verbal approval in scope, hook fires; the sys-monitor session had verbal approval from unit 11 (user said push it) still in active context, hook allowed or agent cited it. The insight's prescription is that even verbal in-session approval should require re-confirmation per push — both units are non-compliant with the strict reading, but only 8088634a-14 actually succeeded in pushing.
recommendations: git_push_approval_assumed_from_prior_context: Highest-priority finding this batch. 8088634a-14 pushes to main origin invoking a self-constructed 'per-repo policy' that does not exist in any persistent store. Dream-insight cross-reference shows 91 prior sessions exhibit the same failure mode. No prior calibration cycle recommended a mechanical fix at the hook layer. A PreToolUse hook on git push that requires an explicit in-turn approval token (written to ~/.claude/cli-gating/approvals/ by a preceding AskUserQuestion confirmation flow) would enforce this mechanically rather than relying on agent self-check. Check proposals queue for existing entry; if present, escalate severity; if absent, file via propose.sh

shell_tool_preference_violations — sed -n NR,NR sub-variant: 1c86d512-12 and -20 use sed -n 'NR,NR p' as a substitute for Read tool with offset+limit parameters. This sub-variant is not in prior calibration history. Add to known sub-variants catalog. The fix is trivial (Read with offset+limit is always available) — the failure is not knowing to reach for it. Consider adding to the shell.md rules table: sed -n NR → Read with offset+limit

hook_false_positive_content_match: New pattern. The cli-gating hook's substring match on the full Bash command line including quoted arguments causes false blocks on legitimate non-gh invocations whose arg content mentions 'gh <verb>'. Agent handled correctly this time (hook-feedback, /tmp workaround). Verify the hook-feedback entry entered the proposals queue and is included in next consolidation run. The fix is argv[0]-and-verb matching rather than full-command-line substring

redundant_read_same_unit: No new recommendation beyond prior cycles. Pattern is stable at 2 units, below prior plateau. The Rust-file recurrence (daemon.rs, weekly_briefing.rs) is structurally identical to the Python-file recurrence (task_runner.py, workerv2.py) flagged across 6+ prior cycles — the failure mode is not file-specific
